title: Build a basic LED circuit
description: Learn electronics basics by safely wiring an LED on a breadboard.

dialogue.text: Hey there! I'm Orion. Let's wire a safe LED circuit and see how simple electronics can be.

Grab a Breadboard (#91), two Jumper Wires (#92), an LED (#93), and a 220 Ω Resistor (#94). Use a USB cable (#95) or battery pack for power, and keep power disconnected while you assemble. Ready to put it together?

Insert the LED so its long (anode) leg reaches the positive rail. Place the 220 Ω resistor from the short leg to the ground rail, then use jumper wires to link the rails to power and ground. Verify the resistor value and LED orientation before applying power, and avoid touching bare metal once the circuit is live. When everything's set, plug in the power and the LED should glow.
Great job! You've built a safe circuit and lit up an LED. Always disconnect power before making changes and store the components for later projects.
